message: Expose prediction output in agent plan (process_triage-yxt.5)

@@ -526,7 +526,7 @@
 {"id":"process_triage-yxt.2","title":"Implement CPU trend analysis and time-to-CPU-threshold","description":"## Context
Part of Phase 12 (Trajectory Prediction). Focus on CPU utilization trends and threshold prediction.

## Problem
CPU usage is noisy. Users need to distinguish:
- legitimate sustained compute (useful heavy compute)
- pathological spin (useful-but-bad)
- abandoned background CPU drain

Trend features can also feed inference (as evidence) and decision theory (expected resource harm).

## Approach
- Input: periodic CPU samples (instant %CPU, CPU time deltas) for an identity.
- Produce:
  - trend label: `increasing | stable | decreasing | bursty | unknown`
  - smoothed estimate (e.g., exponentially weighted moving average)
  - optional forecast: time to exceed a CPU threshold (e.g., 70% sustained)
  - uncertainty / confidence derived from sample count + variance

## Notes
- Prefer robust smoothing (EWMA/median filter) over complex models initially.
- Avoid false precision; show low confidence when variance is high.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Computes stable trend labels on synthetic fixtures (ramp up/down, flat, bursty).
- [ ] Threshold ETA only produced when confidence is sufficient; otherwise “unknown”.
- [ ] Outputs include sample_count, window, variance metrics.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: EWMA, trend classifier.
- Golden tests: synthetic CPU series.
- Property tests: if series is strictly increasing, label not “decreasing”.

 {"id":"process_triage-yxt.3","title":"Implement generic time-to-threshold prediction utilities","description":"## Context
Part of Phase 12. Provide shared utilities to turn trend estimates into time-to-threshold predictions with intervals.

## Problem
We need a consistent, conservative way to express “ETA until X crosses threshold T” for multiple metrics (memory, CPU, FD count, etc.).

## Approach
- Inputs:
  - current value x0
  - trend estimate (slope) with uncertainty
  - threshold T
  - validity constraints (min samples, max horizon)
- Outputs:
  - `eta_seconds` (point estimate) + `eta_interval_seconds` (e.g., 80% or 90%)
  - status: `ok | unknown | already_exceeded | invalid_threshold | insufficient_data`

## Safety/UX Considerations
- If slope interval crosses 0 (trend ambiguous), return unknown.
- Cap maximum ETA horizon (e.g., 30 days) to avoid misleading long forecasts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly handles edge cases (already exceeded, slope≈0, negative slope).
- [ ] Interval math is documented and tested.
- [ ] Produces deterministic output for deterministic fixtures.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: each status branch.
- Golden tests: known slopes/thresholds.

 {"id":"process_triage-yxt.4","title":"Implement per-host baseline fitting and anomaly scoring","description":"## Context
Part of Phase 12. Baselines allow relative anomaly detection and calibrate what “high memory” means on a specific machine.

## Problem
Raw thresholds are brittle across machines. A 2GB RSS process may be normal on one workstation and alarming on another. We need per-host baselines (and optionally per-signature baselines) to contextualize measurements.

## Approach
- Baseline datasets built from historical telemetry partitions (shadow mode / daemon).
- Fit baseline distributions for key metrics:
  - per-host distributions for RSS, CPU, FD count, runtime
  - optionally conditioned on signature category (dev server vs db vs test runner)
- Output anomaly scores:
  - z-score (when appropriate)
  - quantile/percentile rank
  - robust alternatives when distributions are heavy-tailed

## Cold Start
- When insufficient history exists, fall back to conservative global priors and mark as cold-start.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Computes baseline summaries and persists them with a versioned schema.
- [ ] Emits anomaly context fields without leaking sensitive raw strings (respect redaction).
- [ ] Cold-start behavior is conservative and clearly signaled.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: baseline fit math on synthetic samples.
- Integration tests: build baseline from fixture telemetry and query anomaly scores.

-{"id":"process_triage-yxt.5","title":"Expose predictions in agent output (flag-gated, token-efficient)","description":"## Context
Part of Phase 12. Make predictions consumable by humans and agents without overwhelming default output.

## Requirements
- Add `--include-predictions` (or similar) to agent-oriented commands.
- Output JSON fields:
  - per PID/identity: `predictions` object with memory slope, cpu trend, ETAs
  - confidence and diagnostics fields
- Token-efficiency:
  - default output remains compact
  - allow `--fields` / `--only` to select prediction subfields

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Predictions appear in JSON output only when requested.
- [ ] Output schema is documented and validated by tests.
- [ ] Default mode output unchanged (backward compatible within the contract).

## Test Plan
- Schema tests: validate JSON structure against schema.
- E2E: run agent plan on fixture data with predictions enabled.

 {"id":"process_triage-z8he","title":"Implement nohup/disown background process detection","description":"## Section 6.3.5 - Nohup/Disown Detection

**Purpose**: Detect processes started with nohup or disown that are intentionally backgrounded and may be orphaned when the shell exits.

**Detection Patterns**:
- nohup: PPID=1 (orphaned), ignores SIGHUP
- disown: Removed from shell's job table, may or may not be orphaned
- Command pattern: 'nohup' prefix in command line
- File descriptors: stdout/stderr redirected to nohup.out or /dev/null
- Signal mask: SIGHUP ignored (check /proc/<pid>/status SigIgn)

**Distinguishing Intentional vs Accidental**:
- nohup.out exists and growing: Intentional, active
- nohup.out exists but stale: Possibly forgotten
- No output file, high age: Likely forgotten
- Running from /tmp: Likely forgotten

**Implementation Requirements**:
1. `detect_nohup(pid)` - Check for nohup indicators
2. `detect_disown(pid)` - Check job table removal
3. `check_signal_mask(pid)` - SIGHUP ignored?
4. `nohup_output_activity(pid)` - Is nohup.out being written?

**Why This Matters for pt**:
nohup processes are often 'fire and forget'. User started backup 2 weeks ago, forgot about it. High age + orphaned + nohup = likely candidate.

**Test Requirements**:
- Detect nohup from signal mask
- Check nohup.out activity
- Distinguish intentional vs accidental

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the described scope with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).
- [ ] Includes unit/integration/E2E coverage for the highest-risk paths.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:53:03.564381543Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T16:10:18.119382943Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T16:10:18.119382943Z","close_reason":"Implemented nohup/disown background process detection:

## nohup.rs Module
Detection functions implemented:
- detect_nohup(pid) - Full analysis returning NohupResult
- detect_disown(pid) - Detect orphaned + ignores SIGHUP + no nohup cmd
- check_signal_mask(pid) - Check if SIGHUP is ignored
- read_signal_mask(pid) - Parse /proc/<pid>/status for SigIgn/SigBlk/SigCgt
- read_fd_info(pid) - Check stdout/stderr redirection to /dev/null or nohup.out
- check_nohup_output_activity(pid) - Check if nohup.out is active or stale

## Key Types
- NohupResult: is_background, ignores_sighup, is_orphaned, has_nohup_cmd, has_nohup_output
- NohupOutputActivity: Active (modified <5min), Stale, None
- BackgroundIntent: Intentional, Forgotten, Unknown
- SignalMask: blocked/ignored/caught/pending masks from /proc/<pid>/status

## Intent Inference Logic
- nohup cmd + active output = Intentional
- orphaned + stale output = Forgotten  
- orphaned + ignores SIGHUP + no output = Forgotten (likely disown)
- active output without nohup = Intentional (manual redirect)

## Evidence Types Added
- SignalMask (SIGHUP ignored)
- CommandLine (nohup prefix)
- FileDescriptor (stdout/stderr redirection)
- FileActivity (nohup.out freshness)

 {"id":"process_triage-zbd","title":"Add E2E test for main pt workflow","description":"## Purpose
Create comprehensive end-to-end tests that verify the complete pt workflow from scan through kill, including decision memory persistence.

## Parent Epic
Expanded Test Coverage (process_triage-bgd)

## Depends On
- Test helper with mock injection (process_triage-h2y)

## Why E2E Tests Are Critical
Unit tests verify individual functions work. E2E tests verify:
- Components integrate correctly
- Data flows through the entire pipeline
- User-visible behavior matches expectations
- Real-world scenarios work end-to-end

## Test Scenarios

### test/test_e2e_workflow.bats

```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bats

load 'test_helper/common'

setup() {
    setup_test_env
    test_start "$BATS_TEST_NAME" "E2E workflow test"
}

teardown() {
    test_end "$BATS_TEST_NAME" "${BATS_TEST_COMPLETED:-fail}"
    restore_path
    teardown_test_env
}

#==============================================================================
# SCAN WORKFLOW TESTS
#==============================================================================

@test "E2E: scan finds stuck test runner and scores correctly" {
    test_info "Setting up: mock ps with stuck bun test process"
    
    # Create mock ps with a stuck test
    create_mock_ps "$(mock_ps_with_stuck_test 7200)"
    use_mock_bin
    
    test_info "Running: pt scan"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" scan
    
    test_info "Exit code: $status"
    test_info "Output lines: $(echo "$output" | wc -l)"
    
    # Verify exit code
    assert_equals "0" "$status" "pt scan should succeed"
    
    # Verify stuck test is detected
    assert_contains "$output" "bun test" "Should find bun test process"
    
    # Verify scoring (should be KILL or REVIEW based on age)
    assert_contains "$output" "KILL\|REVIEW" "Should recommend action"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: scan with no candidates shows clean message" {
    test_info "Setting up: mock ps with only short-lived processes"
    
    # Create mock ps with only recent/protected processes
    create_mock_ps "$(mock_process 1000 1 1800 64 'vim file.txt')"
    use_mock_bin
    
    test_info "Running: pt scan"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" scan
    
    test_info "Exit code: $status"
    
    # Should succeed but indicate no candidates
    assert_equals "0" "$status" "pt scan should succeed"
    
    # Output should indicate no candidates or be empty
    test_info "Verifying output indicates no candidates"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: scan excludes protected system processes" {
    test_info "Setting up: mock ps with system processes"
    
    create_mock_ps "\
$(mock_process 1 0 9999999 100 '/usr/lib/systemd/systemd')
$(mock_process 2000 1 9999999 50 'sshd: user@pts/0')
$(mock_process 3000 1 9999999 30 '/usr/sbin/cron')
$(mock_process 4000 1000 200000 512 'bun test --watch')
"
    use_mock_bin
    
    test_info "Running: pt scan"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" scan
    
    # Should NOT include protected processes
    assert_not_contains "$output" "systemd" "Should not flag systemd"
    assert_not_contains "$output" "sshd" "Should not flag sshd"
    assert_not_contains "$output" "cron" "Should not flag cron"
    
    # Should include the test runner
    assert_contains "$output" "bun test" "Should flag stuck test"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: scan with mixed processes sorts by score" {
    test_info "Setting up: mock ps with mixed scenarios"
    
    create_mock_ps "$(mock_ps_mixed_scenario)"
    use_mock_bin
    
    test_info "Running: pt scan"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" scan
    
    test_info "Verifying score-based ordering"
    
    # Higher-scored items should appear first
    # Orphan + old should score higher than just old
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

#==============================================================================
# DECISION MEMORY INTEGRATION TESTS
#==============================================================================

@test "E2E: decision memory persists across invocations" {
    skip_if_no_jq
    
    test_info "Setting up: save a kill decision"
    
    # Manually write a decision
    echo '{"bun test --watch": "kill"}' > "${CONFIG_DIR}/decisions.json"
    
    test_info "Verifying decision file exists"
    [[ -f "${CONFIG_DIR}/decisions.json" ]]
    
    test_info "Running: pt history"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" history
    
    # Should show the saved decision
    assert_contains "$output" "bun test" "History should show saved pattern"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: past kill decision increases score" {
    skip_if_no_jq
    
    test_info "Setting up: decision memory with kill pattern"
    
    # Save a kill decision for a pattern
    echo '{"bun test": "kill"}' > "${CONFIG_DIR}/decisions.json"
    
    # Create process matching that pattern
    create_mock_ps "$(mock_process 12345 1000 4000 100 'bun test --watch')"
    use_mock_bin
    
    test_info "Running: pt scan"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" scan
    
    # The process should get boosted score due to past kill decision
    # This is hard to verify directly without seeing the score
    # But we can verify it's flagged
    assert_contains "$output" "bun test" "Process should be flagged"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: past spare decision decreases score" {
    skip_if_no_jq
    
    test_info "Setting up: decision memory with spare pattern"
    
    # Save a spare decision
    echo '{"gunicorn": "spare"}' > "${CONFIG_DIR}/decisions.json"
    
    # Create a gunicorn process that would normally be flagged
    create_mock_ps "$(mock_process 12345 1000 100000 200 'gunicorn --workers 4')"
    use_mock_bin
    
    test_info "Running: pt scan"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" scan
    
    # With spare decision, score should be lower
    # May not appear at all, or should be SPARE recommendation
    test_info "Verifying spare decision affects scoring"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: clear command removes all decisions" {
    skip_if_no_jq
    
    test_info "Setting up: populate decision memory"
    
    echo '{"pattern1": "kill", "pattern2": "spare", "pattern3": "kill"}' > "${CONFIG_DIR}/decisions.json"
    
    # Verify decisions exist
    local count_before
    count_before=$(jq 'length' "${CONFIG_DIR}/decisions.json")
    test_info "Decisions before clear: $count_before"
    assert_equals "3" "$count_before" "Should have 3 decisions"
    
    test_info "Running: pt clear (with mocked confirmation)"
    # Need to mock gum confirm or run in non-interactive mode
    echo 'y' | "$PT_SCRIPT" clear 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Verify decisions cleared
    local count_after
    count_after=$(jq 'length' "${CONFIG_DIR}/decisions.json")
    test_info "Decisions after clear: $count_after"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

#==============================================================================
# HELP AND VERSION TESTS
#==============================================================================

@test "E2E: help command shows all subcommands" {
    test_info "Running: pt help"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" help
    
    assert_equals "0" "$status" "Help should succeed"
    
    # Verify all commands documented
    assert_contains "$output" "scan" "Should document scan command"
    assert_contains "$output" "history" "Should document history command"
    assert_contains "$output" "clear" "Should document clear command"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: version command shows version number" {
    test_info "Running: pt --version"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" --version
    
    assert_equals "0" "$status" "Version should succeed"
    assert_contains "$output" "pt version" "Should show version prefix"
    
    # Verify semver format
    [[ "$output" =~ [0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+ ]]
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: unknown command shows error and hint" {
    test_info "Running: pt unknowncommand"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" unknowncommand
    
    # Should fail
    [[ $status -ne 0 ]]
    
    # Should show helpful message
    assert_contains "$output" "Unknown\|unknown\|help" "Should hint at help"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

#==============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION TESTS
#==============================================================================

@test "E2E: respects PROCESS_TRIAGE_CONFIG environment variable" {
    test_info "Setting up: custom config directory"
    
    local custom_config="${TEST_DIR}/custom_config"
    mkdir -p "$custom_config"
    
    export PROCESS_TRIAGE_CONFIG="$custom_config"
    
    test_info "Running: pt history (should use custom config)"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" history
    
    # Should succeed and potentially create files in custom location
    assert_equals "0" "$status" "Should succeed with custom config"
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}

@test "E2E: creates config directory if missing" {
    test_info "Setting up: empty config path"
    
    local new_config="${TEST_DIR}/new_config_dir"
    export PROCESS_TRIAGE_CONFIG="$new_config"
    
    # Verify doesn't exist
    [[ \! -d "$new_config" ]]
    
    test_info "Running: pt (should create config dir)"
    run "$PT_SCRIPT" help  # Safe command that triggers init
    
    # Verify created (or would be created on first real operation)
    
    BATS_TEST_COMPLETED=pass
}
```

## Setup Requirements

### test/test_e2e_workflow.bats setup
```bash
# At top of file
PT_SCRIPT="${BATS_TEST_DIRNAME}/../pt"

# Verify pt script exists
setup_file() {
    if [[ \! -x "$PT_SCRIPT" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: pt script not found at $PT_SCRIPT" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Scan workflow tested end-to-end
- [ ] Protected process exclusion verified
- [ ] Decision memory persistence tested
- [ ] Clear command tested
- [ ] Help and version tested
- [ ] Unknown command error handling tested
- [ ] Configuration override tested
- [ ] All tests have detailed logging
- [ ] All tests log start/end with pass/fail

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.

 {"id":"process_triage-zdby","title":"Implement conformal prediction intervals","description":"## Overview
Implement conformal prediction for distribution-free prediction intervals and classification sets.

## From Plan Section 4.31

### Regression-Style Conformal Interval (Runtime/CPU)
**Nonconformity Score**:
```
s_i = |y_i - ŷ_i|   (or s_i = -log p(y_i|x_i))
```

**Conformal Quantile**:
```
q = ⌈(n+1)(1-α)⌉-th smallest value in {s_i} over calibration window
```

**Prediction Interval**:
```
[ŷ_{n+1} - q, ŷ_{n+1} + q]
```

**Coverage Guarantee**:
P(y_{n+1} ∈ interval) ≥ 1 - α under exchangeability

### Classification-Style Conformal Set (Process State)
**Calibration**:
For each calibration example i with true label y_i:
```
s_i = 1 - P̂(C=y_i | x_i)   (or NLL)
```

**P-value for Candidate Label c**:
```
s_{n+1}(c) = 1 - P̂(C=c | x_{n+1})
p_c = (1 + #{i: s_i ≥ s_{n+1}(c)}) / (n + 1)
```

**Prediction Set**:
```
{c : p_c > α}
```

Provides finite-sample marginal coverage under exchangeability.

### Time Dependence Handling
- Use blocked or rolling-window conformal (calibration on recent window)
- For strongly non-exchangeable time series, treat coverage as approximate
- Report window size and coverage target in explainability ledger

### Optional: Mondrian / Label-Conditional
Compute p_c using only calibration points with y_i=c for per-class coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Regression conformal intervals implemented
- [ ] Classification conformal sets implemented
- [ ] Rolling window handling for time series
- [ ] Coverage target configurable
